The boxer is known for his power.
He fights with great aggression.
His style is very intense.
The first match was exciting.
He landed one hundred twenty punches.
That number was recorded clearly.
The second match followed soon.
He improved his performance greatly.
He increased his punch count by twenty five percent.
We must calculate the new total.
Twenty five percent of one hundred twenty is thirty.
Adding thirty to one hundred twenty gives one hundred fifty.
So he landed one hundred fifty punches in the second match.
The third match was the final one.
He landed thirty more punches than before.
One hundred fifty plus thirty equals one hundred eighty.
He landed one hundred eighty punches in the third match.
Now we need the grand total.
We add the three match totals together.
One hundred twenty plus one hundred fifty is two hundred seventy.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty is four hundred fifty.
The total number is four hundred fifty.
This sum represents all his power punches.
The calculation is straightforward and clear.
No errors were found in the math.
The boxer showed great consistency.
His power remained high throughout.
The series of matches was long.
Fans watched every single punch.
The aggressive style paid off well.
He dominated the ring completely.
Four hundred fifty is the final answer.
The problem is now fully solved.
